explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' and the 'selfTestPass' condition is met (meaning the self-test procedure has successfully completed) and a 'guiResumeRequest' is received (meaning the GUI asks for resuming ventilation), then it must eventually transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is synonymous with VentilationOff Mode). This captures the requirement that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode must occur when both specified conditions (CONT.4.1 and CONT.4.2) are met.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' and either 'selfTestPass' (the self-test procedure has successfully been completed) or 'guiResumeRequest' (the GUI asks for resuming ventilation) becomes true, then eventually the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is synonymous with 'VentilationOff Mode'). This captures the required transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode upon meeting either of the specified conditions.

The natural language requirement states that the transition from 'Self-Test Mode' to 'VentilationOff Mode' (which is 'StandbyMode') "shall occur" under two specific conditions: when the 'self-test procedure has successfully been completed' ('selfTestPass') OR 'When the GUI asks for resuming ventilation' ('guiResumeRequest'). This implies that while the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually transition to 'StandbyMode', and this transition must be triggered by (or happen concurrently with) one of the specified conditions. The LTL formula G(SelfTestMode -> (SelfTestMode U (StandbyMode & (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest)))) captures this by stating that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must remain in 'SelfTestMode' until 'StandbyMode' becomes true, and at that very moment, the condition (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest) must also be true. This ensures that the transition only happens when the specified conditions are met, and that 'SelfTestMode' persists until that transition. This is a common pattern for expressing mode transitions contingent on certain events.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' AND (the 'selfTestPass' condition is met OR a 'guiResumeRequest' is received), then eventually (F) the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is the 'VentilationOff Mode'). This captures the requirement that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode is triggered by either the successful completion of the self-test or a request from the GUI to resume ventilation.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'Self-Test Mode' (SelfTestMode) and either the 'self-test procedure has successfully been completed' (selfTestPass) or 'the GUI asks for resuming ventilation' (guiResumeRequest) is true, then eventually the system must transition to 'VentilationOff Mode' (StandbyMode). This captures the requirement that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode 'shall occur' under these specified conditions.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in 'SelfTestMode' and the self-test procedure has successfully completed ('selfTestPass') and the GUI asks for resuming ventilation ('guiResumeRequest'), then in the very next state (X), the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode). This directly captures the mandatory transition specified by CONT.4 when both conditions CONT.4.1 and CONT.4.2 are met.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in 'SelfTestMode' AND the self-test procedure has successfully passed ('selfTestPass') AND the GUI requests to resume ventilation ('guiResumeRequest'), then the system must eventually transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is synonymous with VentilationOff Mode). This directly captures the condition that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode shall occur when both specified conditions (CONT.4.1 and CONT.4.2) are met.

The LTL property `G (SelfTestMode & selfTestPass & guiResumeRequest -> X StandbyMode)` captures the requirement. It states that, globally (G), if the system is currently in `SelfTestMode` and both the `selfTestPass` (self-test completed successfully) and `guiResumeRequest` (GUI asks for resuming ventilation) conditions are simultaneously true, then in the very next state (X), the system must transition to `StandbyMode` (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode). This precisely models the 'when' clause for the specified transition.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' and either the self-test procedure has successfully completed ('selfTestPass') or the GUI asks for resuming ventilation ('guiResumeRequest'), then in the very next state (X), the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is described as being the same as VentilationOff Mode). This directly captures the requirement for the transition to occur when either of the specified conditions is met.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually transition to 'StandbyMode' (VentilationOff Mode). Furthermore, this transition can only occur when either 'selfTestPass' (the self-test procedure has successfully been completed) or 'guiResumeRequest' (the GUI asks for resuming ventilation) is true at the moment 'StandbyMode' is entered. The 'U' (Until) operator ensures that while in 'SelfTestMode', the system remains out of 'StandbyMode' until 'StandbyMode' is entered under the specified conditions.
